Ad Text:
Full Time, Continuing / located at La Trobe University’s Bundoora Campus
Salary commensurate to HEO10 $148,049 - $156,840 plus superannuation contributions at 17%
Provide forward thinking leadership in research analytics, leveraging emerging technologies and foster strategic partnerships with external research and ranking bodies

About the position

Research Performance plays a critical role in advancing La Trobe University’s research and innovation impact, global reputation and compliance outcomes. The team leads the development, analysis and communication of research and innovation data for both internal and external stakeholders, ensuring information is accurate, accessible and strategically aligned. Working in close collaboration with Schools, central portfolios, and external bodies including research funders, data providers and ranking agencies the team drives continuous improvement in how research performance is measured, communicated and used to inform strategic decisions.

The Senior Manager, Research Performance provides high level advice to the Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Research and Industry Engagement), academic leaders and professional staff on research performance and its broader context. The role is responsible for delivering insightful analysis to support the implementation of the University’s research and innovation strategy, as well as leading the development and execution of the institutional ranking’s strategy.

Some of your duties will include:

Lead the Research Performance team to deliver high-quality analysis, reporting and continuous improvement in research and innovation performance.
Provide strategic advice to senior leadership on research performance, rankings and evaluation frameworks.
Oversee accurate and timely submissions to government and ranking bodies to support the University’s reputation and compliance.
Support the implementation and monitoring of the University’s research and innovation strategy.
Collaborate with schools, portfolios and central teams to ensure performance insights inform academic and business planning.
Drive the development and maintenance of research systems in partnership with key stakeholders.
Stay ahead of emerging trends in research analytics, including AI/ML, and maintain relationships with key external bodies.
Contribute to leadership across the research office and lead major strategic projects as required.

Skills & Experience:

Postgraduate qualifications and/or extensive experience in strategic policy and organisational leadership.
Strong understanding of higher education and research, including global trends, ranking systems and quality assessment.
Proven ability to lead and implement strategy in complex environments working collaboratively with senior stakeholders.
Skilled in using research performance data to inform decisions.
Strong leadership, negotiation and stakeholder engagement skills.
Excellent communication and representation skills, with experience engaging high level stakeholders.
Demonstrated success in managing complex projects, stakeholder strategies and working across organisational boundaries.
Sound financial, budgeting and change management experience, including technology enabled initiatives.

Please refer to the Position Description for other duties, skills and experience required for this position.

About the role

As a Consultant at MacGregor Advisory Group, you will contribute to delivering high-impact advisory services focused on energy transition and carbon markets. Working with a diverse client base, you will support the development of decarbonisation strategies, emissions reduction initiatives, and carbon market participation across multiple sectors.

This full-time role, based in either our Melbourne or Sydney office, offers the opportunity to collaborate with experienced professionals while playing a key role in shaping practical, forward-looking solutions in a rapidly evolving market.

What you'll be doing

Collaborating with clients to identify and assess their energy transition, decarbonisation, and carbon market challenges and requirements

Designing and delivering tailored solutions to capture opportunities in carbon markets and support decarbonisation, operational efficiency, and sustainable growth

Conducting in-depth market, policy, and competitor analysis across energy and carbon markets to inform strategic decision-making

Preparing high-quality reports, presentations, and proposals to clearly communicate insights, strategies, and recommendations to stakeholders

Supporting the design and delivery of training and capability-building initiatives to enhance clients’ understanding of energy transition and carbon markets

Building and maintaining strong client relationships while developing a network of industry stakeholders across the energy and carbon ecosystem

Continuously developing expertise in energy transition trends, emerging technologies, carbon pricing mechanisms, and industry best practices

What we're looking for

A minimum of 3-9 years of experience in management consulting, strategy, or a related field

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to think critically and creatively

Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the confidence to engage with senior clients

A proven track record of delivering high-quality, impactful work within defined timeframes

A self-motivated and collaborative approach, with the ability to work effectively in a team

Tertiary qualifications in a relevant field, such as business, economics, or engineering

What we offer


At MacGregor Advisory Group, we pride ourselves on fostering a supportive and inclusive environment that encourages both professional development and personal growth. Alongside a competitive salary, we offer clear opportunities for career progression and leadership development, comprehensive training in carbon markets and consulting, flexible work arrangements to support work–life balance, and a collaborative, team-oriented culture with regular social events.

About us

MacGregor Advisory Group is a boutique Corporate Advisory Firm specialising in the Decarbonisation and the Energy Transition. Established in 2025, we have a comprehensive track record build up from decades of experience in our leadership team of helping our clients navigate complex challenges and achieve their strategic objectives. Our team of highly skilled professionals is dedicated to delivering innovative solutions that drive sustainable growth and transformation.

If you're ready to take the next step in your consulting career, we encourage you to apply now for this exciting opportunity.

About the Company

The role sits within the Finance division's data and analytics function, focused on corporate reporting, dashboards (Power BI), and SQL-based analysis across finance, billing, and workforce datasets. It's a senior individual contributor position in an organisation that's still maturing its post-merger operating model, meaning plenty of scope to shape reporting frameworks from the ground up.
  
About the Position
This is a senior reporting analyst role within a large public health organisation's finance and data analytics team. You'll own the delivery of corporate dashboards and performance reports across finance, billing, and workforce data using Power BI and SQL, while acting as a trusted advisor to stakeholders across the business. It's a hands-on, client-facing position — you'll be out amongst operational teams understanding their data needs and translating those into actionable reporting products, not just sitting behind a screen. With the organisation in a period of significant structural change, there's real opportunity to influence how reporting is built and consumed at scale.
  
Job Responsibilities

Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports across finance, billing, and workforce data
Analyse large, complex datasets to identify key business trends and provide actionable insights to stakeholders
Act as a trusted advisor to internal teams, translating data requirements into fit-for-purpose reporting solutions
Build and maintain collaborative relationships with stakeholders across finance, people & culture, and operational teams
Lead the production and upkeep of scorecards and key performance indicators
Respond to ad-hoc data and reporting requests as priorities shift
Mentor and upskill junior members of the team on reporting tools and best practices
Assist with statutory and regulatory data submissions
Identify opportunities to improve internal reporting processes and contribute to building scalable solutions
Support committees and improvement programs through the provision of data, analysis, and subject matter expertise
  
Key Criteria

Tertiary qualifications and/or extensive experience in a relevant business or technology discipline
Minimum 5 years' experience in a similar reporting or data analytics role
Strong proficiency in SQL and Power BI (or equivalent reporting/dashboard tools)
Demonstrated experience working with large, complex datasets with a high standard of data accuracy and rigour
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to engage at all levels of an organisation
Strong analytical and problem-solving capability with sharp attention to detail
Ability to prioritise competing demands and manage a program of work with clear timelines
Comfortable working both independently and as part of a collaborative team
Experience in performance reporting and translating data into meaningful business insights
Exposure to financial, billing, or workforce reporting within a large or complex organisation (desirable)
Experience working in the health or public sector (desirable)
  
Must Haves
1) Power BI & SQL
2) 5+ years in a similar reporting/analytics role
3) Stakeholder management skills — This is front of business stakeholders position.

Ability Roundtable Lead Data Engineer

 

Employment: Permanent, full-time

Location: Hybrid/remote with some travel




The Opportunity

We’re hiring a Lead Data Engineer to own and evolve the data platform behind Australia’s leading disability sector benchmarking service. You’ll be the most senior technical person on the platform, responsible for its architecture, reliability, and future direction.

 We collect financial and operational data from disability and care providers, transform it using standardised business rules, and deliver benchmarking insights that help these organisations improve outcomes for people with disabilities. The platform runs on Microsoft Fabric and processes structured data submissions across multiple benchmarking programs.

This role sits at a critical point in our journey. We are investing in our data capability and laying foundations for the future of disability and care sector benchmarking.  This is a newly created role with real architectural authority and genuine autonomy. You’ll shape technical decisions and work closely with our Data Scientist to build the layer between raw submissions and trusted analytics. And you’ll do it in a sector where the data directly influences how support is delivered to some of Australia’s most vulnerable communities.

You’ll be joining a value-driven organisation that believes great work happens when people are trusted, supported, and given the flexibility to balance life and career. We offer genuinely flexible working arrangements, strong employee benefits, and a supportive environment where people can do meaningful work.

 

We are seeking a Lead Data Engineer with a minimum 5 years’ demonstrated experience working in a data platform management role with experience designing and building data pipelines and ingestion workflows, strong SQL proficiency, experience with data lakehouse and/ or data warehouse architecture and high attention to detail.

If you’re motivated by purpose, enjoy solving meaningful problems, and want to build something enduring in a collaborative, flexible workplace — this role offers the chance to do exactly that.

What you’ll do

 This is an opportunity for a Lead Data Engineer who wants more than just a technical role — someone who is motivated by purpose, enjoys ownership, and wants to help shape something important in the disability sector.

As the technical authority for our data platform, the Lead Data Engineer takes end-to-end responsibility for its stewardship and evolution into the future. This includes platform architecture, security, governance, and ensuring that sensitive data is managed in line with privacy legislation, contractual obligations, and the trust placed in us by member organisations.

 A core focus of the role is building and maintaining data quality and trust. You will define and document data standards, establish best practices, and embed quality controls throughout the data lifecycle — ensuring the platform continues to support reliable benchmarking and meaningful insight.

 A core responsibility of the role is the design and management of the data ingestion layer, including the creation and maintenance of pipelines that process structured flat files in preparation for downstream reporting and analysis. The role also oversees the ongoing development of validation tools to assess data quality at the point of ingestion, ensuring issues are identified and resolved early.

 The Lead Data Engineer owns the storage and curated layers of the platform with the data lake sitting at the centre of the architecture as the primary data environment. This role also encompasses the planned introduction of a data warehouse to support structured reporting needs over time, ensuring both environments remain well-organised, efficient, and scalable as the organisation's needs change.

 Working closely with the Data Scientist, the Data Platform Lead designs and manages the semantic layer that underpins reporting and analysis. This involves defining business metrics, hierarchies, and relationships in a consistent and governed way. The role is responsible for the ongoing performance and reliability of the semantic layer, ensuring it stays aligned with evolving business definitions and remains a dependable source of truth.

 

Key Accountabilities:

Under delegation from, and/or working alongside, the General Manager and Data Scientist, the role will be accountable for:

 

Platform Management and Governance

·        Serve as the technical authority for the Microsoft Fabric data platform, including its overall management, security, and evolution.

·        Ensure complex and sensitive data is handled in accordance with contracts, formal agreements, membership terms, and privacy legislation.

·        Support leadership in identifying and managing platform-level risks, providing technical insight to inform decision-making and ensure appropriate controls are in place.

·        Monitor and maintain platform performance, ensuring the platform remains responsive and reliable for all users.

Data Quality & Standards

·        Define and maintain data standards, best practices, and business rules that form the basis for identifying and resolving data errors.

·        Document and maintain resources such as data dictionaries and pipeline flow diagrams.

·        Investigate and resolve data quality issues as they arise.

·        Develop and maintain validation tools to assess data quality at the point of ingestion, ensuring issues are identified and resolved early.

Data Architecture & Pipeline Engineering

·        Design, build, and maintain data ingestion pipelines that process structured flat files in preparation for downstream reporting and analysis.

·        Design and maintain the organisation's data lake as the primary data environment, with a view to introducing a data warehouse over time.

·        Work closely with the Data Scientist to design and manage the semantic layer, ensuring business metrics, hierarchies, and relationships are defined consistently.

·        Maintain version control across platform code and configuration, ensuring changes are tracked, reversible, and clearly documented.

·        Work with data submitters to manage and improve submission consistency ahead of full automated validation.

·        Continuously identify and implement improvements to the platform, keeping it efficient and aligned with the organisation's changing needs.

What we offer

A competitive salary, commensurate with experience for a lead-level data engineering role in the Australian market. You will also receive superannuation and not-for-profit salary packaging benefits including pre-tax eligible expenses, meal and entertainment benefits, and FBT covered by the organisation. Mobile and internet costs are reimbursed.

 Flexible hybrid/remote working arrangements. A small, collaborative team where your work has visible impact. The chance to shape a growing platform from the ground up, and to do technically meaningful work that improves outcomes for people with disability across Australia.

 

What you bring

Essential

•        Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Engineering, or a related field.

•        5+ years’ experience designing and operating data platforms or pipelines in a production environment.

•        Hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric, or strong skills in a comparable data platform (Databricks, Synapse, BigQuery, Snowflake) with willingness to work in Fabric.

•        Demonstrated ability to design data lakehouse or warehouse architectures, not just maintain existing ones.

•        Strong Python and SQL proficiency. These are your daily tools.

•        Experience building data quality frameworks or validation tooling, not just running data checks, but designing the approach.

•        Working knowledge of data governance, privacy obligations, and ethical data handling, particularly with sensitive or multi-party data.

•        Comfortable operating as the sole technical owner of a platform. You can prioritise, make architectural calls, and explain trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders.

•        Familiarity with Power BI or equivalent BI tools.

•        Willingness to complete pre-employment checks including Worker Screening.

Desirable

•        Experience in the disability, health, aged care, or human services sector.

•        Understanding of the NDIS regulatory and reform environment.

•        Experience working with complex, multi-organisational datasets where data quality varies across contributors.

•        Experience supporting regulatory or sector-level reporting for external stakeholders.

•        Proficiency in Scala, Spark, R, or other languages used in data/analytics contexts.




About the Ability Roundtable

 The Ability Roundtable is a disability sector benchmarking service that supports service improvement through data, peer learning, and collaboration. It provides participating organisations with the opportunity to benchmark their services across Australia through a confidential and trusted platform. Benchmarking helps organisations identify best practices, track performance, and improve the quality of the support they provide.

 The Ability Roundtable currently hosts several well-established benchmarking programs across a range of service areas, including Financial & Workforce, Home & Living, and Allied Health. In recent years, it has expanded its scope to include benchmarking in Quality & Safety and Supported Employment. Each program collects structured data from participating organisations on a regular cycle and applies standardised definitions and business rules to ensure consistency and comparability. The data feeds into the Ability Roundtable’s analytics platform to enable peer benchmarking and interactive reporting.

 The Ability Roundtable’s benchmarking activities are enabled by a purpose-built platform designed to collect, validate, analyse, and present benchmarking data at scale.

About the Company

This organisation is a well-established leader in the utilities sector, delivering critical services to a large and growing customer base. With a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, and future-ready technology, they are investing heavily in data, AI, and advanced analytics.


Their technology function is undergoing a major uplift, with AI positioned as a key driver of smarter decision-making, operational efficiency, and long-term value creation. You'll be joining a business where AI is not just experimental-it's becoming embedded across the enterprise.

About the Role

This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling enterprise AI capability. As Lead Data Engineer, you'll design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes across complex operational environments.




You'll work closely with data leaders, architects, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities and bring high-impact AI use cases to life-from predictive models through to cutting-edge generative AI solutions.


Key responsibilities include:



Leading end-to-end AI/ML solution design, development, and deployment

Establishing and scaling MLOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, lifecycle management)
Building on modern cloud and data platforms (Azure, Databricks)
Embedding AI into enterprise systems to improve efficiency and decision-making
Championing governance, best practice, and responsible AI frameworks
Mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional delivery teams

This role offers a strong mix of strategy and hands-on engineering, with real influence over how AI is adopted across the organisation.

About You

You're an experienced AI/ML engineer with a strong track record of delivering production-ready solutions at scale. You're comfortable operating across both technical and business domains and enjoy turning complex ideas into practical outcomes.


You'll bring:


Strong background in software or data engineering (Python preferred)
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and Databricks ecosystem
Deep experience with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn)
Solid MLOps capability, including model deployment and monitoring
Exposure to generative AI, LLMs, and modern AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain)
Proven ability to deliver AI solutions that drive measurable business value
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership experience

Digital Lead – Intermediary 

Location: Melbourne (Hybrid) 
 

About Guild Insurance and Acerta 

At Guild Insurance, our people feel genuinely connected to our purpose. We are focused on the future, thrive on challenge and learn from one another. We care deeply about our customers and each other, making a real difference by supporting the people and professions our communities rely on. At Guild Insurance we thrive together.

Acerta is Guild Insurance’s intermediary division, delivering insurance solutions supported by an experienced team providing timely broking advice and professional insight. As we invest further in digital capability, this is an exciting time to join Acerta and help shape how brokers engage with us digitally. 

 

The Opportunity 

The Digital Lead – Intermediary leads and shapes the digital experience for Acerta’s broker partners. Acting as the key link between brokers, underwriting teams and internal digital stakeholders, this role ensures our intermediary platforms perform reliably today and continue to evolve in line with business strategy. 

Sitting at the intersection of brokers, underwriting and digital delivery, you will combine hands-on operational involvement with strategic influence. Using broker insight and data, you will help prioritise improvements that enhance usability, efficiency and engagement across the intermediary channel. 

This role offers: 

Genuine influence over Acerta’s intermediary digital platforms 

A rare blend of strategy, broker engagement and hands-on delivery 

Strong organisational investment in digital capability and ongoing transformation 

 

Key Responsibilities 

Act as the primary digital contact for broker partners, ensuring timely issue resolution and a high-quality experience 

Provide day to day support for brokers and underwriters, identifying trends and driving sustainable solutions 

Lead the lifecycle of intermediary digital products, supporting roadmap delivery and continuous improvement 

Monitor digital adoption and platform performance, translating insight into practical enhancements 

Collaborate with internal stakeholders to align daily operations with longer term digital and business strategy 

Conduct market and competitor analysis to inform positioning and improvement priorities 

 

About You 

You enjoy working at the intersection of brokers, systems and technology and have a strong understanding of how brokers transact digitally. You may currently work in an operational or platform focused role within a broking or underwriting environment, where you troubleshoot issues, analyse trends and help improve the digital tools used by brokers and underwriters. Alternatively, you may be in a broker facing role with a strong interest in technology and have played an active role in improving or shaping the systems that support day to day broking activity. 

In either case, you enjoy working across users, products and technology to deliver better outcomes. You bring experience within a general insurance underwriting, broking or intermediary environment, with exposure to digital or underwriting platforms such as Guidewire, Sunrise, Winbeat or Steadfast systems or a purpose built platform. You understand the product lifecycle, can troubleshoot effectively and work closely with technology teams to deliver meaningful improvement, supported by curiosity, analytical thinking and clear communication that turns insight into action. 

 

Why you will love working here 

Hybrid working with two days in the office  and the flex to work from home on other days 

Competitive remuneration with a 15% short term incentive plus paid parental leave and enhanced leave benefits including an extra day of paid leave each year and long service leave 

Staff discounts wellbeing initiatives and access to the Employee Assistance Program for staff and their families 

  

Next steps 

If you are looking to join a company that values your contribution celebrates your success and supports your growth we would love to hear from you. Enquiries can be addressed to Jason  on 0422211297 or you can email recruitment@guildgroup.com.au.  Please do not apply via email

The Opportunity

This 6‑month Senior Business Analyst role focuses on analysing and documenting agent‑initiated payment use cases and understanding how they impact the bank’s issuing platforms, operations, data flows, fraud, disputes, customer visibility, and risk frameworks. You will create end‑to‑end analysis artefacts, map current and future states, identify operational and system gaps, and work with cross‑functional teams to define required changes.

You’ll play a pivotal role in developing a structured action plan and roadmap that ensures the organisation is operationally, technologically and procedurally ready for emerging commerce models driven by AI and automated agents.



Required Skills
7+ years’ experience in card issuing and card payments, with strong knowledge of the full issuing lifecycle

Ability to articulate complex payments concepts clearly to business, technology, product, ops and risk stakeholders

Experience running workshops, managing stakeholders, and aligning multiple cross‑functional delivery groups
Ability to analyse ambiguous, emerging concepts and translate them into clear business and operational impacts


Desirable Skills
Exposure to agentic commerce, AI‑initiated payment flows, LLM‑driven transactions or automated consent models
Experience with AP+, eftpos, Mastercard, Visa, Amex or scheme connectivity
Understanding of payment API flows, tokenisation, COF/MIT transactions, and scheme flagging
Experience with payment platform modernisation, migrations, or uplifts
Knowledge of fraud controls, dispute evidence requirements, scheme rules and ops SLAs
Familiarity with cloud‑based payment architecture or emerging technologies (AI, blockchain, automation)


Benefits
Opportunity to contribute to one of the industry’s first agentic commerce readiness programs
High‑visibility role influencing the future of issuing, ops and customer experience
Partner with senior stakeholders across product, technology, data, fraud, disputes and operations
Work within a collaborative, forward‑thinking team shaping next‑gen payment capabilities
Flexible hybrid working environment

Job Title: Head of Regulatory Pricing and Analysis
Ad Text:
Work with a leading commercial employer for the Canberra region and help steer them through their next period of change.  This organisation is a highly regarded, complex utility operating across regulated infrastructure, retail and corporate environments. With a strong focus on safety, reliability and long-term value, they offer leaders the opportunity to operate at a genuinely strategic level while remaining close to operational delivery.

The organisation is entering a period of transition and is seeking an experienced regulatory pricing leader to provide stability, leadership and strong delivery across an established function.

The Opportunity

This is a critical leadership role at the centre of regulatory pricing for the electricity network distribution business, responsible for setting tariffs, shaping pricing strategy and translating complex quantitative outputs into clear commercial and regulatory outcomes.
 
This position sits within a high-performing and collaborative team and reports to the General Manager. It combines leadership, technical oversight and hands-on delivery, with three direct reports across economic regulation.
 
The role is 90 per cent focused on regulatory pricing for the distribution business, with a small exposure to retail pricing. It plays a central role across regulatory cycles, including annual submissions and longer-term five-year reviews for both the gas and electricity networks.
 
You will oversee regulatory pricing models and ensure that strategic intent is accurately reflected in the numbers, while also translating complex modelling into clear, effective communication for executive, board and regulatory audiences.
 
The Role

Key responsibilities and outcomes include:

Provide expert regulatory pricing, financial and strategic advice to senior executives and the Boards
Lead the development, review and application of regulatory pricing models, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives
Oversee annual pricing resets and support longer-term regulatory reviews across electricity and gas networks
Shape tariff structures and pricing strategies, including incentive schemes, connection policies and regulatory frameworks
Act as a key interface with regulators, as well as shareholders and broader stakeholders
Lead regulatory submissions, advocacy and stakeholder engagement across the pricing lifecycle
Provide leadership, coaching and oversight to a small, high-calibre team while remaining hands-on in delivery
Translate complex quantitative analysis into clear, concise and commercially relevant insights
The Ideal Person

You will likely bring experience from a network utility, regulator or advisory environment, such as energy, water or telecommunications. Candidates from consulting firms or regulatory bodies are also encouraged to apply. This role may suit someone who is ready to step up into a broader leadership position.
 
You will demonstrate:

Strong experience in regulatory pricing, modelling or economic regulation
The ability to interpret regulatory frameworks and apply them to pricing and revenue outcomes
A highly developed commercial mindset, with the ability to link data, strategy and business performance
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with experience engaging at executive and board level
A collaborative leadership style and the ability to operate effectively in a small, high-impact team
Strong stakeholder engagement capability, both internally and externally
Salary / Rate

Total remuneration package of $250k - $280k, with flexibility based on experience
Flexible working arrangements, including a mix of office and work from home
Four-day or five-day working week
Free on-site parking
A supportive and flexible culture, including accommodation for family commitments
This is an excellent time to join, ahead of the next major regulatory cycles, and to play a key role in shaping pricing strategy and outcomes for a critical Canberra business.
 
How to Apply

To apply online, please click on the appropriate link.
 
Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Alan Larby on 02 6108 4878 quoting ref no. 21448

Hands-on experience with Workato or similar platforms (Power Platform, Boomi, Appian)
Strong understanding of API integration (REST/SOAP), data governance, and security practices
Proficient with Microsoft 365 and process mapping tools (e.g., Lucidchart)
Experience in Agile delivery and requirements management tools
Exceptional stakeholder management, problem-solving, and communication skills
Desirable: SQL, data engineering fundamentals, CRM/ERP system knowledge

If you also believe that everyone should be able to achieve their financial potential, then you’ll love contributing to CMC Markets’ company vision of providing the ultimate trading experience. Seize the opportunity to progress your career in the fintech industry by joining a global leader in CFD trading and share investing. Grow yourself personally and professionally with a company that’s growing with you, by global expansion and product offering.

As a Financial Risk Manager your analysis will reach the desks of senior leaders. Your models will inform how the business hedges positions, manages liquidity, and responds to volatility. In a team of three reporting directly to the Head of Financial Risk Management and Liquidity, there is no middle layer between your work and the decisions it drives. That kind of visibility is rare at this level.

You will also build deep technical skills that set you apart in the market. Stress testing, scenario analysis, quantitative modelling, and reporting, this role stretches all of them. CMC's dual CFD and Stockbroking businesses mean the exposures you manage are varied and genuinely complex, giving you breadth that single-product businesses simply cannot offer.




Why You'll like working here

Just some of the reasons you’ll love working here include:


Leave benefits: Accrue 5 additional annual leave entitlements each year called My Days, as well as enjoying 5 extra annual leave days once you pass 12 months. Enjoy your birthday off to spend time with family and friends, on us! Utilise a free annual charity day to give back to your community.


$0 Trades: Staff & family trading accounts with CMC Markets Invest have access to the best brokerage rates in the market - $0 trades for all domestic and international equity orders!




Continuous learning: Our L&D offering covers both technical and performance training, ensuring you're always learning new skills.




Unlimited access to a dedicated benefits & wellbeing hub where you can save money through various retailer discounts, and make use of a variety of resources that will enhance your physical and mental wellbeing




Collaboration: We believe that great things happen when smart people work together, that’s why we’ve made the decision to work five days in the office.
 

Office perks: Help yourself to free breakfast cereals, condiments, and coffee to fuel your workday. Our culture club provides opportunities for everyone whether you’re into fitness, pizza making classes, socialising or bringing out your competitive side in trivia or lawn bowls!




What you’ll be working on

Day to day, you will:

Monitor and report on key risk metrics and KRIs across CMC's CFD and Stockbroking businesses, ensuring material exposures are reviewed, approved, and escalated appropriately.

Produce risk reports, dashboards, and management information for senior leaders and risk committees with a focus on clarity and relevance, not just compliance.

Develop, maintain, and enhance risk models; run market analysis, scenario analysis, and stress testing to assess potential impacts on financial positions.

Drive continuous improvement in risk reporting and analytics, including building out new capabilities to strengthen monitoring and oversight.

Build strong working relationships across Dealing, Finance, Treasury, Compliance, and senior management providing the risk insights that support real decisions.

Support and manage project initiatives within the Financial Risk team, delivering against agreed objectives.




What will help you be successful?

You bring solid financial risk experience ideally from a stockbroking or trading environment and you are comfortable working with complex data and presenting your findings clearly to senior stakeholders. Specifically, you will need:

Demonstrated experience in financial risk management within a stockbroking, trading, or similar financial services environment

Strong analytical and quantitative capabilities, with a relevant risk or finance qualification preferred

Sound knowledge of derivatives and financial instruments

Advanced Excel skills; experience in Python and/or SQL is highly regarded

Familiarity with industry systems including CHESS, Palion, GBST, and Iress

High attention to detail and a proactive, inquisitive approach to problem-solving

The ability to manage competing priorities and meet deadlines

Professional certification such as FRM, CFA, or PRM is desirable — but not a prerequisite

You must have full working rights in Australia.




If you have been working as a Market Risk Manager, Risk Reporting Manager, or Quantitative Risk Specialist, we would love to hear from you.




The things we live by

CMC Markets is powered by our people. We are an equal opportunities employer and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace, where our people feel confident to be themselves, feel valued and are able to do their best work. We don’t just value differences and unique perspectives, we seek them out and we invite them in, because we know it will lead to better outcomes for everyone.


We’re always on the lookout for great people, so even if you don’t feel you are a 100% fit, apply with your up-to-date CV and we’ll consider you for this role and any other suitable opportunities across the business. 




 We look forward to hearing from you! 





Please be vigilant when applying for jobs online. CMC Markets will never request personal identification details, such as Date of Birth or Tax File Number prior to an offer being made. If your initial application is successful, regardless of whether you've applied directly or through an approved recruiter, you will always liaise with a representative from CMC's Talent Acquisition team. We will only discuss a role you have formally applied for via phone or email (CMC Markets email address); and never via Facebook

Grant Thornton Australia is one of the world's leading independently-owned and managed accounting, advisory and consulting firms.

Our culture is underpinned by a commitment to our clients, people and communities, and our promise to ‘Reach for Remarkable’ by providing proven expertise, principled execution, and personalised experience.

In Australia, we’re proud to support the careers of over 1,500 remarkable people. With six offices nationally and partner firms in more than 145 markets, wherever you want to go, we can take you there.

The opportunity

As part of Grant Thornton’s national Sustainability Reporting Advisory team, you will play a senior leadership role at the forefront of sustainability reporting and regulatory change in Australia and globally. You will lead complex client engagements, support the development of high performing teams and contribute to the continued growth and maturity of the practice as organisations respond to rapidly evolving sustainability reporting expectations.

The Sustainability Reporting Advisory team is on a strong growth trajectory, driven by mandatory sustainability and climate related financial disclosures, heightened board and executive focus, and increasing integration of sustainability considerations into financial, risk and strategic decision-making.

We support organisations to apply sustainability reporting standards and regulatory requirements – including AASB S1 and S2, IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards and related frameworks – in a way that is practical, proportionate and aligned to business strategy and value creation. Operating as a truly national team, we collaborate closely with assurance, tax and advisory specialists to deliver integrated, commercially focused sustainability reporting solutions.

Our work spans the full maturity spectrum, from early-stage readiness and executive education through to first time and ongoing reporting, helping clients embed sustainability into governance, financial reporting, risk management and performance frameworks.

With mandatory climate related financial disclosures now legislated in Australia, the team plays a critical role in technical leadership, capability building and market education. We support clients as they prepare to report, while also strengthening sustainability reporting capability across Grant Thornton through internal technical leadership and collaboration with assurance and other service lines.

Senior Manager value proposition

As a Senior Manager in Sustainability Reporting Advisory, you bring depth, judgement and calm leadership to complex sustainability reporting engagements at a time of significant regulatory change. You combine strong technical capability with proven delivery leadership, enabling clients to navigate new reporting obligations with confidence while building sustainable internal capability. You play a critical role in leading teams, managing managers and setting the standard for quality, consistency and professionalism across the practice, and bring the credibility to support—and, where of interest, increasingly lead—client pursuits that strengthen trusted relationships and contribute to the firm’s growth.

Your role

As a Senior Manager, you will be a leader within the Sustainability Reporting Advisory practice. You will be responsible for the end-to-end leadership of significant client engagements, the management and development of managers and senior team members, and active contribution to the delivery excellence and strategic direction of the practice.

You will work closely with Partners and Directors to build and maintain strong client relationships, support business development initiatives and lead the development of high-quality proposals. This includes shaping client propositions, contributing to scope and approach development, and supporting the effective transition from proposal to delivery.

In addition to client delivery, you will play an important role in shaping service offerings, strengthening internal capability and supporting go to market initiatives. This role requires a confident, mature and experienced professional who is comfortable operating at executive and board level, managing senior teams and delivering high quality outcomes in a dynamic and evolving regulatory environment.

Key responsibilities

Client leadership and delivery

Lead the end-to-end delivery of complex sustainability reporting advisory engagements, with primary accountability for technical quality, delivery outcomes and client experience.

Act as a trusted adviser to senior management and executive stakeholders on sustainability reporting obligations, readiness and implementation challenges.

Oversee engagement planning, resourcing and delivery across multiple concurrent engagements, ensuring work is delivered efficiently and to Grant Thornton’s quality standards.

Provide senior technical direction on the interpretation and application of sustainability reporting standards, legislation and regulatory guidance to client specific circumstances.

People leadership – managing the managers

Lead, coach and develop Managers and Senior Associates, setting clear expectations and supporting performance, capability development and progression.

Build depth and resilience in the team by developing future leaders and strengthening technical, client facing and delivery capability.

Foster a strong, inclusive and collaborative team culture aligned to Grant Thornton’s national operating model and values.

Practice contribution and commercial support

Support business development activities primarily through high quality delivery, trusted client relationships and technical credibility.

Contribute to proposal development, including shaping scope, approach and deliverables, in collaboration with Partners and Directors.

Assist with engagement scoping, pricing inputs and transition from proposal to delivery to support clarity, efficiency and execution discipline.

Contribute to the refinement of sustainability reporting service offerings and internal delivery approaches based on practical client experience.

Technical leadership and market profile

Provide senior review and oversight of client deliverables, ensuring outputs meet regulatory, governance and assurance expectations.

Contribute to internal training, technical guidance and capability building initiatives across the firm.

About you

You have:

Deep experience interpreting and applying sustainability reporting requirements, supported by a strong foundation in financial reporting, accounting and assurance concepts.

A proven ability to translate complex regulatory and technical requirements into clear, pragmatic advice for senior executives and boards.

Significant experience in a professional services or advisory environment, leading client engagements and managing senior team members.

Strong delivery and commercial judgement, with experience overseeing engagement economics and client relationships.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the confidence and presence to engage credibly with senior stakeholders.

You are:

A confident, mature and experienced professional who brings sound judgement, perspective and calm leadership to complex situations.

Client focused and outcomes driven, with a strong sense of accountability for quality and delivery.

Comfortable managing competing priorities across multiple engagements while maintaining standards and momentum.

A collaborative leader who builds trust, develops others and contributes positively to a national, multidisciplinary team.

Motivated by building capability – in clients, in teams and across the firm – in a rapidly evolving sustainability reporting landscape.

Qualifications and experience

CA or CPA qualified, or tertiary qualifications in a related discipline.

Significant experience in financial audit, accounting advisory, sustainability reporting, ESG or sustainability assurance and advisory.

Strong knowledge of IFRS and AASB standards, with demonstrated capability to lead others in technical application.

Strong knowledge of Australian Sustainability Reporting Standards (AASB S1 & S2) and the Greenhouse Gas Protocol, with demonstrated capability to lead others in technical application.

Knowledge of other sustainability reporting frameworks such as IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards, TCFD, ESRS, or GRI is highly regarded.

Experience working with assurance teams and an understanding of audit and assurance processes is advantageous.

Career development

Grant Thornton offers a clear pathway to Director and Partner for high performing Senior Managers. You will be supported through leadership development, coaching, national and global networks, and opportunities to shape the future of the Sustainability Reporting Advisory practice.

Location

This is a national role. Applications are open to candidates based in Sydney, Melbourne or Brisbane.

Reach your remarkable

At Grant Thornton we do things differently because we understand that when you strive for better and care about what you do remarkable things are possible. We’re a dynamic and authentic mix of backgrounds, perspectives and ways of thinking. We’re driven by our shared purpose – we care for our people, clients and communities and support them to thrive.

With us, you’ll be exposed to challenging and rewarding opportunities – building your confidence and capabilities at every step. You’ll be supported, motivated and inspired by a team of passionate and caring collaborators and leaders. You’ll be empowered to build a career path that’s tailored to you and encouraged to make a meaningful difference. And we’ll make sure the excellence you deliver and the impact you make is always seen, felt and celebrated.

9-day fortnight with no salary reduction

Funded professional membership and study assistance

Up to 26 weeks’ paid parental leave | paid secondary carers’ leave

Fully funded gym membership | Health Insurance discounts

And many more!

1 of the two days per week in office spent with your team.  



The IT Business Analyst plays a critical role in helping business teams to articulate their needs and for IT to understand, prioritise and implement its pipeline of change requests.

This is a full-time, fixed-term position for one year.

Collaborates with stakeholders across the College to clarify and articulate needs and identify, justify, and ensure implementation of appropriate technological solutions that align with College goals.
Assists requestors, solution development stakeholders to prepare business cases, product roadmaps, clarify project scope, identify solution and project risks and impacts.
Collaborates with stakeholders to understand current practices, define, engineer and document target processes
Aligns processes with requirements and solutions
Leverages College system solutions such as M365, Atlassian suite, Dynamics 0365 modules, Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse and Power BI.
Supports testing and change adoption activities.
Produces specifications for application features, workflows, and data models
Produces release notes to ensure stakeholders understand process changes and new solutions.
Ensures compliance with College security, risk, project management and governance frameworks when scoping, designing and assisting deployment of solutions

In order to be considered for this role we are seeking the following skills, experience and attributes:

 Essential:

Over 8 years of experience working as a Business Analyst with highly developed business analysis skills.
Experience writing business cases, engineering and re-engineering and modelling processes and developing business architecture documentation.
Ability to manage multiple internal projects, prioritise tasks, and meet deadlines
Experience working in Waterfall and Agile/Scrum environments.
Experience working within Project Management frameworks
Product Management Frameworks
Knowledge of DevOps practices and tools, such as Azure DevOps, Jira Projects for managing application lifecycle and deployments.

Desirable:

Microsoft Certified in Dynamics 365 Fundamentals and PL-200 Power Platform Functional.
Experience implementing Dynamics 365 and Power Platform solutions (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, Dataverse).
Skilled with Atlassian and Microsoft 365 product suite implementations.
Proficient in Power BI reporting and dashboard development.
Knowledge of industry-specific Dynamics 365 solutions across Education, Membership‑based organisations, and NFP sectors.

 




Please submit your cover letter and CV 

For additional information please find the job description listed here: ba-it-position-description.pdf

If you would like any further information, please email recruitment@racp.edu.au. This email address is for enquiries only.

We encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible as will be shortlisting and interviewing as applications are received. Our ad may close earlier than the specified closing date should a suitable candidate be found.

This role is currently being managed by our internal People & Culture team, and we are not accepting applications from recruitment agencies.

Job Title: Senior Commercial Finance Analyst - Supply Chain
Ad Text:
The Role

David Jones exists to inspire like no other. As we continue delivering our ambitious Vision 2025+ transformation agenda, we are committed to creating inspired careers where our people can thrive.

We are seeking a Senior Commercial Finance Analyst – Supply Chain Transformation to join our Commercial Finance team. This role plays a pivotal part in enabling commercial decision-making through insightful financial analysis, strategic evaluation, and partnering across key transformation initiatives within our Supply Chain operations.

You will be embedded within project teams and operational stakeholders, supporting major business initiatives with data-driven insights, financial modelling, and performance tracking to ensure the successful delivery of transformation outcomes.


What YOUR DAY LOOKS LIKE

Partner with project and operational teams to understand challenges and opportunities across major supply chain initiatives and ongoing operations.
Deliver robust financial analysis, including P&L modelling, scenario analysis, feasibility studies, and post-implementation reviews.
Support the evaluation of business cases by analysing financial impacts, risks, and mitigation options for proposed initiatives.
Develop and maintain financial models to support planning, forecasting, and performance tracking across transformation projects.
Prepare and present decision-support materials and insights for executive-level stakeholders.
Monitor actuals vs. projections, analyse variances, and provide actionable recommendations.
Support the budgeting and forecasting process for transformation projects and operational teams.
Identify opportunities for process improvement or innovative financial approaches that enhance decision-making.
Collaborate closely with the broader finance team and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure alignment with strategic and operational objectives.

What YOU’LL NEED TO THRIVE

Tertiary qualification in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline; CA/CPA qualified (or near completion).
Strong experience in commercial finance, ideally within a retail or FMCG supply chain environment.
Proven experience in business partnering with non-finance stakeholders and providing commercial insights.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience in financial modelling and scenario analysis.
Excellent communication and presentation skills - able to convey complex financial insights clearly.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver to tight timelines within a fast-paced transformation environment.
A collaborative mindset, with a proactive approach to driving value and continuous improvement.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver to tight timelines within a fast-paced transformation environment.
A collaborative mindset, with a proactive approach to driving value and continuous improvement.
initiatives.
Outstanding communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey complex financial concepts to non-finance stakeholders.
Strong financial modelling skills with the ability to handle complex, multi-variable scenarios.
Ability to lead planning process for multi-functional projects balancing financial and non-financial considerations as well as clearly articulating assumptions
Ability to prioritise workload and manage multiple competing projects with varying timelines.
Ability to build strong relationships across various business units and work effectively in cross-functional project teams.

Why WORK FOR US?

Unique opportunity to be part of a highly engaged, successful team, focused on the transformation of an iconic Australian brand
Opportunity to directly impact major business decisions and shape the future of a leading retail organisation
A competitive remuneration package including performance-based incentives
Hybrid working arrangements in office and from home that provide appropriate work/life balance
Parental leave policy of 18-weeks paid leave for the primary carer, and 3-weeks paid leave for the supporting partner
Generous employee discounts across David Jones and access to partner benefits
An additional day of leave for your birthday along with time to support charitable work
Opportunities to support community partnerships across our Corporate Social Responsibility program
Be a member of a company committed to sustainable practices, driving change in the retail landscape
Ongoing training and development to pursue individual ambitions and career goals

About Us

Since 1838, David Jones’ limitless quest for innovation and progress established the brand as Australia’s original influencer in fashion and lifestyle. Having revolutionised the way Australians shop, David Jones’ creation of a social centre inspired and created lasting memories for past, present and future generations. Today, our vision to inspire Like No Other drives us to continue this legacy in our ambitious purpose to be the destination that inspires, with experiences and services Like No Other.


Our Thriving cultural pillars inspire our people to deliver on our vision and purpose. We are Customer Obsessed; curious to understand and dedicated to delighting them with seamless solutions as one team. We care for our people, customers, partners and community by creating Inclusive environments through belonging and respect. We Empower with implied trust to act with integrity, value our unique skills and be accountable for our decisions. We constantly seek Innovative ways of improving, changing and exploring ways that we can inspire.

Job Title: Principal Capital Finance Business Partner | Perth
Ad Text:
About BHP 


At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.

Come and be a part of this success.

About the Role


The Principal Capital Finance Business Partner acts as a trusted advisor to the business, providing commercial insight, constructively challenging assumptions, and supporting informed decision making across the capital lifecycle. The role has a strong focus on capital productivity, governance, prioritisation, and ensuring alignment between strategic objectives and capital deployment.

Key Responsibilities:

Partnering with project and asset leaders to support capital planning, prioritisation, and performance management
Providing investment evaluation and commercial advice on capital projects
Stewardship of the capital portfolio, including cash prioritisation and governance
Driving improved capital efficiency and value outcomes through insight and challenge
Engaging across a broad range of stakeholders to support effective decision making
Leading and delivering capital reporting, including portfolio performance insights, cash flow, and capital productivity reporting, to support governance, prioritisation, and informed decision making

About You


To be successful in this role, you will be able to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders while maintaining a high attention to detail. You will be naturally curious with a continuous improvement mindset, inspiring challenge through highly effective communication and strong interpersonal skills. You will build constructive relationships and credibility across a range of stakeholders and cross functional teams.

Key experience and attributes:

Experience as a finance professional with strong commercial acumen and relevant finance exposure
Well developed commercial and analytical skills, including the ability to translate complex financial analysis into clear, actionable insights for decision makers
Demonstrated experience in reviewing capital investment, investment evaluation, or decision support, including providing commercial advice across the capital lifecycle and supporting disciplined capital allocation decisions
Postgraduate qualifications such as a Master’s degree or MBA, CPA, CFA, or CA are highly desirable
About Our Process 


At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks. 

If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your BHP email address or apply via our internal jobs portal. 

Supporting a Diverse Workforce  
 


The size, stability and magnitude of our business not only provides significant opportunity for professional development, but also attractive salary packages with performance-based bonuses and a best-in-class employee share program. We know there are many aspects of our employees' lives that are important, and work is only one of these, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, other extended leave entitlements and parent rooms.  

At BHP, we know that we are strengthened by diversity. We are an Equal Opportunity employer that is committed to making BHP a safe and inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive and be at their best every day. We are focused on creating a workforce that’s more diverse and represents the communities where we work and live. providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. We are an Equal Opportunity employer and recognise that true diversity includes gender, age, race, disability status, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, education levels, and many more aspects of your identity.

BHP is committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you have a disability, we know that it may be helpful for us to adjust our process to make it equitable for your individual situation. If you would like to reach out to someone about your situation and our recruitment process, please email us at inclusion@bhp.com.
